chunks.memory_unit_text: In the FuelLabs/sway release v0.66.5, the release note includes a rewrite of the TypeEngine for performance, robustness, and simplicity; the addition of storage domains; profiling support; transmute intrinsics; a revert with log function in the standard library; Forc.toml metadata support; a function for generating bytecode identifier; and various dependency bumps and cleanups.

chunks.title: v0.67.0
chunks.raw_text:
Repository: FuelLabs/sway
Release: v0.67.0
Tag: v0.67.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-03-13T16:14:34Z; created_at: 2025-03-13T15:25:49Z
Release notes:
## What's Changed
* Trait map optimizations by @tritao in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7007
* Merge std and core libraries by @SwayStar123 in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/6729
* Promote experimental features by @ironcev in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7016
* Bump to v0.67.0 by @IGI-111 in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7017
## Breaking Changes
* `core` and `std` libraries have been merged
* Storage keys are now [calculated differently](https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/issues/6701)
* The `Eq` trait has been split into `Eq` and `PartialEq`
* conversions from `Bytes` to `b256` are now fallible
We recommend using the [new `forc migrate` tool](https://docs.fuel.network/docs/forc/plugins/forc_migrate/) to address these breaking changes in your codebase.
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/compare/v0.66.10...v0.67.0
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the FuelLabs/sway release v0.67.0, the release note includes trait map optimizations, merging of std and core libraries, promotion of experimental features, and a version bump to v0.67.0. Breaking changes include the merging of core and std libraries, differently calculated storage keys, splitting of the Eq trait into Eq and PartialEq, and fallible conversions from Bytes to b256.

Release notes:
## What's Changed
* Fix register allocator non-deterministic output across platforms by @tritao in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7422
* Allow trait impls to inherit default associated constants by @tritao in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7409
* fix: various typos around codebase to unblock ci by @kayagokalp in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7432
* Symptom-fix associated const shadowing itself by @ironcev in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7429
* fix: also create deployment artifacts for chunked deployments by @kayagokalp in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7426
* chore: bumpt rust version to 1.90 by @kayagokalp in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7427
* fix: formatter use newline interference by @kayagokalp in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7431
* feat: introduce fuel-telemetry macros by @kayagokalp in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7295
* forc-node wasm32 removal by @zees-dev in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7436
* Stabilize `new_hashing` by @IGI-111 in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7437
* Bump to 0.70.0 by @IGI-111 in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/pull/7438
## Breaking Changes
Hashing has received a significant change to alleviate security issues as detailed in https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/issues/7256.
The `new_hashing` feature is now enabled by default which will result in dynamically sized collections now producing hashes that include size to reflect their shape.
If you have code that uses canonical hashes as part of its logic or relies on the stability of sway produced hashes you may be affected.
Make sure to use `forc migrate` to check if you are. If necessary you may disable the change for now with `--no-experimental new_hashing`, however this option will be removed in a future release.
**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/FuelLabs/sway/compare/v0.69.6...v0.70.0
